sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart, currently positioned 10th in the league with 44 points, host 1. FC Heidenheim, who sit in 16th place with just 26 points, in Round 31 at the MHP Arena in Stuttgart. This matchup sees a mid-table side facing a team battling relegation, promising an intriguing clash. The referee for this game, Tobias Welz, averages 4.0 yellow cards and 0.18 red cards per match, indicating a likelihood of disciplinary actions during the game.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last encounter on December 15, 2024, VfB Stuttgart defeated 1. FC Heidenheim 3-1 away from home, with Maximilian Mittelstädt earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.5. Over the last 5 matchups between these teams, Stuttgart have dominated with 3 wins, while there has been 1 draw and 1 win for Heidenheim. Notably, Stuttgart have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 head-to-head games, suggesting Heidenheim might find the net despite their struggles.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart come into this game with a strong home trend, having seen over 2.5 goals in 7 of their last 8 home matches and both teams scoring in 8 of their last 10 home games. They’ve also been first to score in 8 of their last 10 home fixtures, showing early dominance. On the other hand, 1. FC Heidenheim are in poor form, with 3 consecutive losses, no goals scored in those matches, and no clean sheets in their last 3 away games, highlighting their defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - VfB Stuttgart
sections.text: For Stuttgart, Angelo Stiller stands out as a key midfielder with an average Sofascore rating of 7.4 and 8 assists this season, ranking high in creative output with 8.60 expected assists. Maximilian Mittelstädt, a defender with a rating of 7.34, has also been pivotal with 6 assists. Up front, Ermedin Demirović leads with 13 goals, exceeding his expected goals of 12.69, while Deniz Undav adds flair despite underperforming with 8 goals against an xG of 9.87. Young defender Finn Jeltsch also impresses, ranking 5th in the league for ground duels won at 69.4%.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - 1. FC Heidenheim
sections.text: For Heidenheim, forward Marvin Pieringer is the main attacking threat with 7 goals, though he’s underperformed against an xG of 8.59. Midfielder Leo Scienza, ranked 3rd in the league for fouls drawn per game at 2.4, has contributed 3 goals and 2 assists with a solid rating of 7.02. Defender Patrick Mainka anchors the backline with a rating of 7.08, while Marnon Busch ranks 3rd in the league for long balls as a percentage of total passes at 10%, showcasing their direct style.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Style
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart excel in possession, ranking 4th in the league with an average of 57.31%, and are prolific in creating chances, ranking 3rd with 104 big chances created this season. They also rank 4th in corners per game at 5.03 and penalty area entries per game at 31.53, indicating an attacking approach. Conversely, 1. FC Heidenheim struggle defensively, often conceding first in 5 of their last 7 away matches, and their goal distribution shows a reliance on late strikes, with 20% of goals coming between minutes 76-90.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart will miss midfielder Jamie Leweling, who has 2 goals and 2 assists this season, along with defenders Josha Vagnoman and Anrie Chase, potentially weakening their squad depth. 1. FC Heidenheim are without midfielder Mathias Honsak, who has scored 5 goals, and forward Budu Zivzivadze with 2 goals, which could further dent their already struggling attack. These absences may tilt the balance further in Stuttgart’s favor.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Standout Performances
sections.text: Angelo Stiller has been a standout in recent head-to-heads against Heidenheim, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 8.175 across 3 starts, with 1 goal and 1 assist. Stuttgart’s knack for late goals is notable, with 29.41% of their goals coming between minutes 76-90, which could be crucial if the game remains tight. Additionally, both teams have a trend of matches with less than 4.5 cards in 9 of their last 10 head-to-heads, suggesting a relatively clean game despite referee Tobias Welz’s average of 4 yellows per match.
